findings.title: Run registry write is not durable before independent session launch
findings.detail: `Write` uses `os.WriteFile` followed by `os.Rename` but never fsyncs the temp file or the parent directory. This registry is the only durable pointer used to adopt independent tmux sessions after daemon SIGKILL; a power loss or kernel crash after the daemon decides `useIndepSession=true` can leave the surviving session with no durable `.harmonik/runs/<runID>.json` record. On restart the adoption path cannot distinguish a live independent run from no run, which can lead to duplicate dispatch or stale in_progress recovery racing live work.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the same temp-write discipline as the crew registry: create a sibling temp with `O_EXCL`, write, `Sync`, close, rename, then fsync the `.harmonik/runs` parent directory before returning success. Treat any sync/close failure as a failed registry write so callers fall back before launching independently.

findings.title: Corrupt run records are silently hidden from recovery
findings.detail: `List` suppresses every `Load` error, including malformed JSON and permission/read errors, and returns the remaining records as if the directory were healthy. Callers use `List` to protect in-flight runs and to adopt dead sessions; hiding a bad record means a torn or otherwise corrupt registry entry can make the daemon forget an active run instead of failing conservative. This is especially risky because `Write` currently lacks fsync, so crash-created bad state is part of the threat model.
findings.suggested_fix: Only ignore `ErrNotFound` for a file that vanished between `ReadDir` and `Load`. For parse/read errors, return an error or a conservative stub tied to the filename so adoption/sentinel logic treats the run registry as unknown rather than empty.

findings.title: List trusts the JSON run_id instead of the filename
findings.detail: `List` derives `runID` from `<filename>.json` for loading, but then returns the unmarshaled `Record` without checking that `r.RunID == runID`. Downstream recovery removes records via `Remove(projectDir, rec.RunID)`, so a mismatched or corrupted payload leaves the actual file behind forever; if the payload contains path separators, `recordPath` will also resolve outside `.harmonik/runs`. A single bad record can therefore break adoption cleanup or delete the wrong registry file.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate run IDs at the package boundary as UUID/base-name values. In `List`, require the payload run_id to match the filename, or overwrite `r.RunID` with the filename-derived value before returning; never pass untrusted payload text into `recordPath`.
